rationale.R1.1: The section advances a specific contribution claim ('first RL approach to directly optimize LLM calibration'); reconstructing that claim is prerequisite to any substantive review.
rationale.R1.2: The argument chain—Brier-score reward → ECE reduction → cross-domain transfer → reward-shape ablation—must be checked for internal consistency; gaps or contradictions between subsections would undermine the section.
rationale.R1.3: Precise quantitative claims are central: ECE 0.18→0.06 (TriviaQA), 0.21→0.09 (MMLU), accuracy delta ≤0.4%, ECE <0.10 OOD, 1.7% accuracy drop under log-loss. These must align with the described experimental setup; misalignment is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.4: An explicit 'to our knowledge, no prior work' novelty claim is made; evaluating it against the cited literature (and plausible omissions) is a central review responsibility.
rationale.R1.7: Five named citations (Christiano 2017, Ouyang 2022, Kadavath 2022, Tian 2023, Lin 2022) are used to frame both motivation and novelty; accuracy and adequate coverage of the calibration literature require verification.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review cover substantive correctness—logical consistency, evidence–claim alignment, and the novelty claim—or focus primarily on writing quality and clarity, or both?
